tasks.description: Extend NextAuth types for access_token in JWT
steps: Add JWT interface extension to control-panel/src/types/next-auth.d.ts
JWT interface includes accessToken?: string field
TypeScript compiler accepts token.accessToken in callbacks without errors

tasks.description: Persist Discord access_token to JWT for API calls
steps: In jwt callback, persist account.access_token to token.accessToken on initial sign in
Token contains accessToken field available in signIn callback
Access token is not exposed to client session (security)

tasks.description: Add conditional OAuth scopes based on env var configuration
steps: When DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID is set, OAuth consent includes 'guilds' scope
When DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID is set, OAuth consent includes 'guilds.members.read' scope
When neither env var is set, no additional OAuth scopes are requested
Scopes are added to DiscordProvider authorization params dynamically
Scopes are read from process.env at module load time (restart required for changes)

tasks.description: Add signIn callback to verify guild membership
steps: signIn callback returns true when user is authorized (member of guild)
signIn callback returns false when user is unauthorized (triggers redirect to /auth/error?error=AccessDenied)
When DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID is set, call Discord API GET /users/@me/guilds/{guild_id}/member
Non-members (404 response) are denied with AccessDenied
API errors (5xx) deny login (fail-closed)
Discord API fetch has 5-second timeout configured
Timeout errors are caught and logged as Error level, trigger AccessDenied
Rate limited (429) retries once with 1-second delay then denies
When X-RateLimit-Remaining < 10, log warning with remaining count
Error scenarios logged appropriately (Info for access denied, Error for API failures)

tasks.description: Add role verification within guild member check
steps: When DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_ID is set (with guild ID), verify user has that role
Users in guild without required role are denied access
Users with empty roles array (no roles in guild) are denied when role restriction is enabled
Role check handles null/undefined roles field gracefully (treat as empty array)
Role check is skipped if DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_ID is not set
Role-only config (ROLE_ID without GUILD_ID) logs warning and skips restriction (any user can log in)

tasks.description: Verify backward compatibility with no env vars
steps: When neither DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID nor DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_ID is set, any Discord user can log in
No additional OAuth scopes requested when restriction disabled
Existing deployments work without configuration changes
No breaking changes to existing NextAuth session structure
Sessions created before restriction enabled will fail on next login (expected, documented)

tasks.description: Add env vars to control-panel configuration files
steps: control-panel/.env.example contains DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID with description
control-panel/.env.example contains DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_ID with description
Both env vars documented as optional
.env.example notes that control panel must be restarted for scope changes to take effect

tasks.description: Add env vars to Kubernetes secret template
steps: infra/secrets.yaml minions-control-panel secret has DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID with description and REPLACE_ME placeholder
infra/secrets.yaml minions-control-panel secret has DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_ID with description and REPLACE_ME placeholder
Deployment already uses envFrom to load all secret keys (verify no explicit env: entries needed)

tasks.description: Document OAuth scope requirements in discord-app setup
steps: docs/setup/discord-app.md documents the guilds and guilds.members.read OAuth scopes
docs/setup/discord-app.md has 'Prerequisites for Guild Restriction' section
Prerequisites section explains bot must be in guild for guilds.members.read to work
Prerequisites section explains how to verify bot membership (check Members list in Discord)
docs/setup/discord-app.md has troubleshooting section for 403 Forbidden errors
docs/setup/discord-app.md documents restart requirement when enabling restrictions
docs/setup/discord-app.md warns existing users will see new OAuth consent screen when restrictions enabled
docs/setup/discord-app.md warns DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_ID is ignored without DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID

tasks.description: Update README with control panel env var documentation
steps: README.md Control Panel env var table includes DISCORD_ALLOWED_GUILD_ID
README.md Control Panel env var table includes DISCORD_ALLOWED_ROLE_ID
Both vars documented as optional with clear descriptions
README.md notes control panel restart required when adding guild/role restrictions

patterns: NextAuth configuration: control-panel/src/lib/auth.ts (jwt and session callbacks)
